title: kyllinga caespitosa var. pumila
intro: Kyllinga caespitosa var. pumila, also known as dwarf water pygmyweed, is a small, aquatic plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, Kyllinga caespitosa var. pumila is often used as a tea or decoction, with some cultures also employing it in traditional remedies and folk medicine.
